era_context: The Middle Bronze Age II in the Levant and Egypt; the 12th Dynasty of Egypt under Amenemhat III. In Mesopotamia, Hammurabi's Old Babylonian state is consolidating power. The Indus Valley Civilization is in its mature phase. In China, the Xia Dynasty (traditional chronology) is in its later period.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Sub-Saharan Africa: no written records; archaeological evidence is limited.
The Americas: no written records; archaeological context is not recoverable at annual resolution.
Northern Europe and the Steppe: sparse written documentation; primarily archaeological inference.
Southeast Asia and Oceania: minimal written records; archaeological evidence is fragmentary.

events.title: Amenemhat III's reign: continued prosperity and administrative consolidation
events.description: Amenemhat III's reign continues with documented prosperity, administrative stability, and building projects. Inscriptions record the continuation of mining expeditions, trade networks, and the maintenance of Egypt's centralized bureaucratic state. The Fayum region remains a focus of development. Egypt's economy and borders remain stable.

sources.name: Temple inscriptions and administrative records from the 12th Dynasty

events.certainty_note: Amenemhat III's reign is well-attested. The year -1964 falls within his documented reign; specific annual events are rarely distinguishable.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: Documentation for this year is minimal and concentrated in Egypt. Written records are limited to administrative and religious texts. The archaeological record provides material evidence but cannot resolve events to annual precision. Most regions of the world have no written documentation for this period.

graph_edges.note: Amenemhat III's reign continues; part of the same period of Egyptian prosperity.
